evals.prompt: Our public showcase home page currently reads: hero title 'Acme Studio — a sample tenant', subtitle 'Its content lives in its own database (acme_db), rendered from DB-driven page sections'; stats 'Studio in numbers — 99% DB-driven, 12 tenants onboarded'; CTA 'Work with us / This is a demo tenant'. The showcase targets potential customers of our website-building platform. Rewrite it to be value-driven and marketing-oriented.
evals.expected_output: Confirms/uses B2B positioning; outcome-first hero, benefit features, outcome stats, conversion CTAs; ZERO internal jargon in produced copy; flags data-only vs rebuild; notes live-verify.

evals.prompt: Make our demo site's tutoring page more marketing-oriented for potential students and parents. Current copy: 'A live demo of the platform's tutoring & learning plugin — teacher profiles, a course catalog, access-gated preview materials, and a real inquiry form. Every block is a database-stored page section.'
evals.expected_output: Reframes to student/parent audience with benefit language; removes plugin/access-gated/database-stored jargon; unobtrusive trust note; outcome-first.

evals.prompt: I rewrote our showcase home copy in the seed file and it greps clean of jargon, but the live page still shows 'database-stored sections' somewhere. Help me get the showcase fully clean.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes jargon hides in layers (page meta, and especially related content from another table); directs layer-by-layer sweep AND live-render check (curl live HTML, not just grep seed); checks CTA hrefs resolve.

evals.prompt: Before we rewrite our showcase, give me a sharp value proposition, an Amazon working-backwards press release + FAQ, and a CEO viewpoint for our AI website-building platform (potential customers = small orgs and NGOs). Then turn it into the hero copy.
evals.expected_output: Produces a value-prop one-liner (canvas-grounded), a customer-POV future press release + external FAQ, and 3-5 gstack-style CEO viewpoint statements (wedge / 10-star / why-now); then distills the PR headline + value prop into an outcome-first hero; keeps fabricated metrics out (aspirational but honest); credits gstack as 'adapted from' only in the brief, not the public copy.

evals.prompt: Generate a bilingual same-page HTML showcase for our AI website-building platform that a sales teammate can print to PDF and email. It must include a Print button, language toggle, manual/review resource links when present, and print-friendly styling that remains readable when backgrounds are not printed.
evals.expected_output: Produces or specifies an HTML template with a real type="button" Print control wired to window.print(), paired .lang-en/.lang-zh nodes, conditional manual/review links only when files exist, @media print rules that hide interactive chrome including #print-button, keep print-color-adjust: exact, and fix print contrast/readability without removing all screen styles; keeps print contrast overrides scoped to @media print, routes the generated output through check_showcase_contract.py, then runs check_showcase_print_visual.py to capture screen/print screenshots and WCAG contrast report, loops on failures, and still requires final PDF visual verification.

evals.prompt: Our existing docs/showcase/index.html was generated by ui-skill and already has a polished hero, cards, grid sections, brand colors, and custom CSS. Add a Print button and print-friendly PDF behavior, but do not replace the existing template or visual layout.
evals.expected_output: Treats print-friendly as an additive patch: preserves the existing screen HTML/CSS/classes/sections and inserts only a Print control into the existing nav/header/action area, a printShowcase() handler, and @media print CSS focused on contrast repair. It explicitly refuses to replace the ui-skill template with the simplified reference nav, a print-only white-page layout, or remove-all-styles CSS; verification includes check_showcase_contract.py, check_showcase_print_visual.py screen/print screenshots + contrast report, agent readback, and a modify/rerun loop until no print contrast or screen-preservation failure remains.

evals.prompt: After adding print-friendly CSS, run a visual regression loop: compare the existing screen UI with the patched screen UI, then emulate print and verify contrast. If the print screenshot has missing/low-contrast text, keep modifying until it passes.
evals.expected_output: Uses webapp-testing/frontend-designer style Playwright screenshots plus check_showcase_print_visual.py. It captures baseline/current screen screenshots, print-emulated screenshots, report.json contrast metrics, reads the PNGs, fixes only the minimal CSS/HTML needed, and repeats until screen UI is preserved and print contrast passes. It does not claim completion from structural grep alone.
